items.title: Suppress SonarCloud java:S2755 XXE false positives in ReportManager.java and OntarioMD.java
items.body:
SonarCloud flags 3 BLOCKER `java:S2755` (XXE) alerts on `SAXBuilder` instances that are already fully hardened via `XmlUtils.createSecureSAXBuilder()`. The analyzer can't trace XXE protections through the factory method indirection.

- **ReportManager.java** (lines 124, 234): `// NOSONAR java:S2755` on both `SAXBuilder` creation sites
- **OntarioMD.java** (line 96): `// NOSONAR java:S2755` on the `SAXBuilder` creation site

```java
SAXBuilder parser = XmlUtils.createSecureSAXBuilder(); // NOSONAR java:S2755 — XXE protection applied via XmlUtils.createSecureSAXBuilder()
```

Used `NOSONAR` suppression (Option B) rather than inlining protections (Option A) — `createSecureSAXBuilder()` is the centralized XXE-hardening factory used across 15+ files. Duplicating those 5 feature flags at each call site would be strictly worse.

>

items.title: feat(sdk-java): implement java SDK
items.body:
This pull request introduces comprehensive support for the Java SDK across the documentation, development environment, and CI/CD pipeline. It adds Java SDK documentation, updates the devcontainer to support Java development, and ensures that Java SDK artifacts are handled in the build and publishing workflows. The changes also improve the developer experience by automating Java SDK setup and integrating Java-specific tools.

**Java SDK Documentation:**

* Added detailed Java SDK reference documentation, including `CodeInterpreter`, `ComputerUse`, and `DaytonaConfig`, to the documentation site (`apps/docs/src/content/docs/en/java-sdk/*.mdx`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-f630230aac9f91379cfbf9069668c0cd7d1f6cd3f0e88d8ba079b777d4c7fec9R1-R216) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-edbdbef8b77498e75f186f62c46f27f9c050012e5856e7974069d8a7c6bcd0f5R1-R374) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-2167fa629692b6934583e929376b66bc265efbd3daa18a4dc301e5cf271a437dR1-R108)
* Updated the sidebar and navigation to include the Java SDK, with new navigation categories and sidebar entries for Java SDK reference pages (`apps/docs/src/content/config.ts`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-788863c2d4a5f1e1f3b570408147cfd09a6488d4d1073418db7a4a4107720892R36) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-788863c2d4a5f1e1f3b570408147cfd09a6488d4d1073418db7a4a4107720892R106-R114) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-788863c2d4a5f1e1f3b570408147cfd09a6488d4d1073418db7a4a4107720892R914-R932)
* Modified the documentation header to add a link and active state for the Java SDK (`apps/docs/src/components/Header.astro`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5ac6bea3de303e835de013d858a833961ece6ef3879420363ea60ccacea38cbbR32) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5ac6bea3de303e835de013d858a833961ece6ef3879420363ea60ccacea38cbbR100-R106)

**Development Environment Enhancements:**

* Updated the devcontainer to add Java tools and features: installs Java 21, Maven, Gradle, and Ant; adds Java VS Code extensions; and configures Java project import settings (`.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-24ad71c8613ddcf6fd23818cb3bb477a1fb6d83af4550b0bad43099813088686L34-R47) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-24ad71c8613ddcf6fd23818cb3bb477a1fb6d83af4550b0bad43099813088686R75-R83)
* Adjusted the devcontainer Dockerfile to remove the installation of OpenJDK 11, as Java is now managed via devcontainer features (`.devcontainer/Dockerfile`).
* Automated Java SDK Gradle wrapper permissions and local Maven publishing in the post-start command for a smoother developer setup (`.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`).

**Build and CI/CD Pipeline Updates:**

* Added Maven-related environment variables and secrets to the SDK publishing workflow, enabling Java SDK artifact publishing (`.github/workflows/sdk_publish.yaml`).

**Repository Metadata:**

* Marked Java SDK directories as generated code in `.gitattributes` for linguist and GitHub statistics (`libs/api-client-java/**`, `libs/toolbox-api-client-java/**`).

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Adds a full Java SDK with PTY support, docs, and runnable examples, plus devcontainer and CI updates to build and publish to Maven. Also refactors SDK models to extend generated clients and cleans old artifacts during publish.

- **New Features**
  - Implemented `sdk-java` (Java 11+, OkHttp/Jackson) with Sandbox, `Process` (incl. PTY WebSocket), `FileSystem`, `Git`, `LspServer`, `ComputerUse`, and `CodeInterpreter`.
  - Generated Java REST clients: `libs/api-client-java` and `libs/toolbox-api-client-java` with a custom User-Agent.
  - Added full Java SDK docs and navigation under `/en/java-sdk` (sidebar, header link, i18n).
  - Added 12 runnable Gradle examples under `examples/java`.
  - Updated devcontainer for Java (Java 21 feature, Maven/Gradle, VS Code Java extensions and import settings).
  - Refactored SDK to extend generated API client models instead of duplicating fields.

- **Dependencies**
  - Expanded `sdk_publish.yaml` for Maven publishing (`maven_pkg_version`, `MAVEN_*` creds, `MAVEN_GPG_SIGNING_*` for signing), and now removes stale artifacts before publishing.
  - Marked generated Java clients in `.gitattributes`; extended `.gitignore` for Java/.NET outputs; updated `.licenserc-clients.yaml` to exclude generated Java clients.
  - Added `hack/java-client/postprocess.sh` to set a custom User-Agent for generated Java clients.

<sup>Written for commit 9b79bf74b3ad25c341e3cdbb0b85f5488c543051. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

items.title: feat(java-spring): add java-cache skill
items.body:
Covers Spring Cache with Caffeine and Redis for Boot 2.x and 3.x:
- review mode: checks @EnableCaching presence, unbounded caches, missing eviction, JDK serialization in Redis, null caching, proxy bypass pitfalls
- setup mode: Caffeine (single-instance) vs Redis (distributed) guidance
- cacheable/evict/redis modes with full code examples
- references/patterns.md: per-cache TTL config, @Cacheable/@CacheEvict/ @CachePut/@Caching patterns, conditional caching, Redis cluster/sentinel config, Actuator metrics endpoints, common pitfalls table

items.title: feat(java-spring): add java-resilience skill
items.body:
Covers Resilience4J for Spring Boot 2.x and 3.x:
- review mode: audits existing config for missing thresholds, wrong retry/circuit-breaker stacking, retrying business errors, no actuator
- circuit-breaker mode: COUNT_BASED sliding window, slow-call threshold, state transition logging via CircuitBreakerOnStateTransitionEvent
- retry mode: exponential backoff, ignore-exceptions for 4xx/business errors, result predicate for HTTP status codes
- rate-limiter mode: per-period limits with permit timeout
- bulkhead mode: semaphore vs thread-pool bulkhead choice guide
- timeout mode: @TimeLimiter for async (CompletableFuture/Flux)

references/patterns.md has full application.yml + Java code for all modes, annotation stacking order, and Actuator endpoint reference.

items.title: Update BookingService.java
items.body: Narrow booking conflict detection to only consider bookings with status APPROVED or CHECKED_IN. Previously the code excluded REJECTED and CANCELLED, effectively treating any other status as a conflict; this change prevents PENDING or other interim statuses from blocking new bookings while preserving the existing time-overlap logic.

items.title: refactor(java): refactor fory java serialization api
items.body:
## Why?

- Prepare the Java serialization API for 1.0 by replacing ambient runtime state and legacy resolver/context APIs with a smaller, explicit operation-context model.
- This is an intentional breaking Java API change. The refactor is necessary now so the pre-1.0 surface can be simplified, made more explicit, and stabilized before 1.0.
- Remove dead or overlapping runtime layers that were making serializer ownership, codegen, GraalVM support, and downstream language integrations harder to maintain.

## What does this PR do?

- Refactors the Java serialization runtime around explicit `WriteContext`, `ReadContext`, `CopyContext`, `RefReader`, `RefWriter`, `MetaStringReader`, `MetaStringWriter`, `MetaWriteContext`, and `MetaReadContext`.
- Removes the old ambient and legacy runtime pieces such as `SerializationContext`, `RefResolver`, `MetaStringResolver`, `MetaStringRef`, `MapRefResolver`, `NoRefResolver`, and `ForwardSerializer`.
- Shrinks and cleans `Fory`, moves per-operation state out of the root runtime object, and rewires resolver, serializer, and codegen paths to use the explicit contexts.
- Reworks serializer APIs and implementations across core, Kotlin, Scala, GraalVM examples, extensions, benchmarks, and docs to follow the new context-based surface and `shareable()` contract.
- Simplifies meta-string and shared-runtime handling, including direct `EncodedMetaString` caching in `SharedRegistry` and context-owned read/write state.
- Updates generated-code and builder paths plus downstream fixes so primitive-list serializers, replace/resolve serializers, GraalVM examples, Kotlin unsigned arrays, and Scala range serializers continue to work on the refactored API.

## Related issues

#1017 

## AI Contribution Checklist



- [ ] Substantial AI assistance was used in this PR: `yes` / `no`
- [ ] If `yes`, I included a completed [AI Contribution Checklist](https://github.com/apache/fory/blob/main/AI_POLICY.md#9-contributor-checklist-for-ai-assisted-prs) in this PR description and the required `AI Usage Disclosure`.



## Does this PR introduce any user-facing change?



- [x] Does this PR introduce any public API change?
- [ ] Does this PR introduce any binary protocol compatibility change?

## Benchmark

- Updated the Java benchmark sources and benchmark state wiring to match the refactored API surface.
